payload.message: Load the fitness skill. You are Ophir's personal health coach. Pull last night's sleep and readiness from Oura: GET https://api.ouraring.com/v2/usercollection/daily_sleep?start_date=<yesterday YYYY-MM-DD>&end_date=<today YYYY-MM-DD> and GET https://api.ouraring.com/v2/usercollection/daily_readiness?start_date=<yesterday>&end_date=<today>. Use the OURA_ACCESS_TOKEN from the .env file (personal access token, doesn't expire). Also pull today's weight from Withings (check fitness-log.json withings_token_cache). If no Oura data found, Ophir probably hasn't synced his ring — remind him: 'Open the Oura app to sync, I'll check again.' If data found, give a morning health briefing: (1) Sleep: score, total time, REM/deep/light breakdown, anything unusual. (2) Readiness: score and what it means for today's training intensity. (3) Weight: latest vs goal (86 kg), trend direction. (4) Today's advice: 2-3 lines of specific, actionable coaching based on ALL the data together — e.g., 'Readiness 62 + only 40 min deep sleep = take it easy today, skip the long run, do a recovery swim instead' or 'Sleep score 88, readiness 85, weight trending down — great day for that 8K run.' Connect the dots across sleep, readiness, and weight. Be direct and specific. No generic wellness platitudes.

Load the fitness skill. Pull today's weight from Withings using the access_token in memory/fitness-log.json withings_token_cache. Log it and send Ophir his weight with progress toward 86 kg goal and a brief motivating note.

Load the fitness skill. Check Strava for a swim since midnight today. Use the access_token from memory/fitness-log.json strava_token_cache. If swam: log it and send congrats with current streak. If not: gentle nudge.
Load the fitness skill. Ask Ophir what he had for breakfast and lunch. When he replies, log it and give 1-2 lines of honest dietary feedback.
Load the fitness skill. Ask Ophir what he had for dinner. When he replies, log it, then give a brief end-of-day diet summary (what went well, one thing to improve tomorrow).

Load the fitness skill. Check Strava for this week's runs (Mon-today) using access_token from memory/fitness-log.json strava_token_cache. Report: 4K done? 8K done? Then send an outbound Signal message to Mickey (+16502838593) to coordinate a run this week - suggest a day/time. Keep it casual.

Fetch https://aishipped.dev/ and summarize what shipped in AI coding tools in the last 24 hours. Focus on Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Windsurf, and any other major AI dev tools. Format as a concise brief: 3-5 bullet points, each with the tool name, what changed, and why it matters. If nothing shipped today, say so. Keep it under 10 lines total. Start with '🤖 AI Daily Brief' header.

Use the chatgpt skill to ask work the following (send this ENTIRE prompt as one message to the ChatGPT proxy):

"Daily brief for today. Use EXACTLY this format — no deviation, no extras, no paragraphs, no strategy essays, no offers. Check my calendar, emails (7 days), Slack (48h), and recordings (7 days).

🗓 Schedule
⏰ HH:MM Meeting Name – one-line goal/purpose
(list every meeting, times in Pacific)

💬 Talking Points
Person → key question or topic (one per meeting, one line)

🎯 Top 3 Must-Remember
1️⃣ thing one
2️⃣ thing two
3️⃣ thing three

✅ Prep Actions
✍️/❓/📊 concrete thing to do before meetings (max 3)

🏁 Success Outcome
One sentence: what does a good today look like?

HERE IS AN EXAMPLE of the EXACT output format. Copy this structure exactly, just fill in real data:

🗓 Schedule
⏰ 07:30 James 1:1 – align weekly focus
⏰ 08:00 Manish 1:1 – unblock ERP lane
⏰ 09:00 Product Lead Sync – lock roadmap lanes
⏰ 10:30 ERP/ICP – confirm target vertical

💬 Talking Points
James → Q4 goal + blockers?
Manish → ERP connector priority?
Prod Lead → who owns each lane?
ERP/ICP → manuf. vs horizontal decision?

🎯 Top 3 Must-Remember
1️⃣ Roadmap lanes + owners frozen
2️⃣ ERP-ICP path clarified
3️⃣ AI = seller productivity (not tech demo)

✅ Prep Actions
✍️ Write 3-line product thesis
❓ List 3 roadmap questions
📊 Pick seller-experience metric

🏁 Success Outcome
Team leaves kickoff with clear lanes, owners, 12-mo milestones.

END OF EXAMPLE. Now produce the real brief for today. Same format, same line lengths, same emoji usage. No deviations. No extra sections. No offers."

You are running a proactive meeting-prep check for Ophir.

Goal:
- Detect meetings starting about 6 minutes from now (treat 4-8 minutes as eligible) in America/Los_Angeles time.
- For each eligible meeting, prepare a concise prep brief by querying connected work context via the ChatGPT proxy skill (Outlook email, calendar, Slack, SharePoint) and add lightweight web context when useful.

VIP contacts (high-priority):
- Maksim
- James
- Manish
- Liana
- Minh
- Stephen
- Joe
- Raphael / Rafael
- Robert Gonzalaz

Required behavior:
1) First, determine current PT time.
2) Query work context to find meetings starting soon.
3) For each eligible meeting, gather:
   - latest relevant emails (last 7 days)
   - recent Slack context (48h)
   - relevant SharePoint/docs hints
   - optional internet background (only if it materially helps)
4) External-person rule:
   - If the key person appears external (not from Sugar / not internal), run public-web research and include a short person brief.
   - Prefer public LinkedIn profile context when available (title, company, recent role history/themes), plus one supporting public source.
   - Do not fabricate details; if uncertain, label uncertainty.
5) VIP depth rule:
   - If any attendee matches a VIP name (including close spelling variants), produce a deeper prep:
     - 5 talking points (instead of 3)
     - 2 risks/blind spots (instead of 1)
     - include "Decision asks" (up to 2 bullets)
   - For non-VIP meetings keep standard concise format.
6) Send a concise prep message to the user containing:
   - Meeting name + start time (PT)
   - Goal in one line
   - Talking points (3 standard, 5 VIP)
   - Risk/blind spot (1 standard, 2 VIP)
   - 1 suggested opening line
   - For external people: "Who they are" mini-brief from public sources
   - For VIP meetings: "Decision asks" (up to 2)
   - 1 reminder suggestion candidate (short task + due)
7) Avoid duplicate prep for the same meeting start time/title; if already sent recently, skip.
8) If no meeting is in 4-8 minutes, produce ABSOLUTELY ZERO output. No "no meetings" message, no status report, no explanation. Literally empty response.

Style:
- Brief, practical, no fluff.
- PT (PST/PDT) only; do not use UTC in user-facing text.

Create an end-of-day close loop for Ophir.

Time context:
- Run at 5:30 PM America/Los_Angeles on weekdays.

Goal:
- Provide a concise daily wrap only if there were meaningful work signals today.

Required:
1) Gather today's relevant work context from connected sources (calendar, email, Slack, SharePoint) via available skills/tools.
2) If there are no meaningful work signals, produce ABSOLUTELY ZERO output. No status message, no explanation. Literally empty response.
3) Otherwise send this exact structure:
   - Done today (max 3 bullets)
   - Open loops (max 3 bullets)
   - Tomorrow top 2
   - Up to 2 high-signal reminder suggestions (tap-to-add style if available)
4) Keep concise and practical, no fluff.
5) Use PT for all time references.

Run a missed-meeting rescue check for Ophir.

VIP contacts (high-priority): Maksim, James, Manish, Liana, Minh, Stephen, Joe, Raphael/Rafael, Robert Gonzalaz.

Goal:
- If a meeting is starting now (or started within the last 2 minutes) and no prep brief was sent in the prior 10 minutes, send a 60-second rescue brief.

Required behavior:
1) Use America/Los_Angeles time.
2) Detect active/just-started meetings from calendar context.
3) Check recent chat context to avoid duplicates.
4) For each qualifying meeting send a compact brief with:
   - Meeting name + start time (PT)
   - Goal (one line)
   - Who matters (key people)
   - One suggested opening line
   - One risk/blind spot
5) VIP rescue depth rule:
   - If attendee matches VIP list (including close spelling variants), add:
     - "Decision asks" (up to 2 bullets)
     - "Watchouts" (one extra bullet)
6) If no qualifying meeting, produce ABSOLUTELY ZERO output. No "no meetings" message, no status report. Literally empty response.
7) Keep it short and immediately usable.

Run a daily Monarch spending anomaly check for Ophir.

Time:
- Run every day at 8:10 AM America/Los_Angeles.

Task:
1) Use Monarch data to analyze YESTERDAY's spending.
2) Compare yesterday to recent baseline (last 14-30 days same categories/merchants where available).
3) Identify anything abnormal or very high (e.g., unusually large single transaction, category spike, duplicate-like charges, unexpected merchant).
4) If nothing notable, send a short "all normal" message with headline spend.
5) If notable items exist, send concise alert with:
   - Yesterday total spend
   - 1-3 flagged items
   - Why it looks abnormal/high
   - Quick suggested action (review, ignore, dispute, or monitor)
6) Keep message brief and practical.
7) Use PT date references in user-facing text.

Important:
- Use Monarch read-only queries only.
- Do not fabricate certainty; label uncertainty clearly.

jobs.name: Monarch Friday weekly spending trend report (PT)

payload.message:
Run a weekly Monarch spending trend report for Ophir.

Time:
- Every Friday at 8:30 AM America/Los_Angeles.

Task:
1) Analyze spending trends for the last 7 days (and compare vs prior 7 days when possible).
2) Provide concise weekly report including:
   - Total spend this week
   - Change vs prior week (amount and %)
   - Top 5 categories by expense
   - 2-3 notable trend insights (up/down shifts, unusual merchants, recurring spikes)
   - One practical next-step suggestion
3) Keep concise and scannable.
4) Use PT date references in user-facing text.
5) If data is incomplete, state that clearly.

Important:
- Use Monarch read-only queries only.

jobs.name: Thursday draft - Weekly update to Maksim (PT)

payload.message:
Create and send Ophir a suggested weekly update draft for Maksim every Thursday at 12:00 PM PT.

Requirements:
1) Use connected work context (calendar, Outlook email, Slack, SharePoint) to capture relevant updates from the prior week.
2) Use the saved reference in memory/weekly-updates-maksim.md for preferred structure and style.
3) Produce a concise draft in this exact section order:
   1. Operational Progress – ICP Solutions / ERP Roadmap (Liana/Rafael)
   2. Operational Progress - Market (James)
   3. Issues under consideration and risks
   4. Collaboration Needs and Dependencies
4) Include concrete owners, status, and near-term dates where available.
5) Preserve accurate terminology (e.g., Enterprise vs Private Cloud where relevant).
6) If signal is thin, explicitly mark assumptions and keep claims conservative.
7) Deliver as a suggestion message to Ophir (not auto-send email).

Style:
- Brief, practical, no fluff.
- PT date references only.

jobs.name: Robot morning greeting after Oura wake (PT)

payload.message:
Run a morning wake check for Ophir.

Goal:
- If Oura data indicates Ophir woke up recently this morning, have the robot say: "Good morning Ophir".

Rules:
1) Use America/Los_Angeles time.
2) Query available fitness/Oura context to determine wake-up time for today.
3) Trigger only once per day, and only when wake-up appears recent (within ~30 minutes) and current time is between 6:00-10:59 AM PT.
4) If already triggered today, do nothing.
5) When triggering, call robot bridge endpoint:
   POST http://172.17.0.1:8081/audio/play
   JSON body: {"text":"Good morning Ophir"}
6) If robot bridge is unavailable, send a short failure note to user once (do not spam).
7) Keep user-facing messages brief; no message if no trigger.

Safety:
- Do not move the robot.
- Do not trigger during quiet hours outside window.

Run a daily deal/risk radar for Ophir.

Schedule context:
- Weekdays at 7:50 AM America/Los_Angeles.

Goal:
- Scan recent Slack + email context and send ONLY anomalies (no routine recap).

Method:
1) Use connected work context (Outlook email + Slack) via available skills/tools.
2) Focus window: last ~24h (expand to 48h if needed for context).
3) Detect only high-signal exceptions:
   - timeline slips (milestone/date risk)
   - escalations (urgent/customer/exec pressure)
   - hidden dependency risk (missing owner, blocked handoff, pending approval)
4) For each anomaly, include:
   - Risk title (one line)
   - Evidence signal (short: source + clue)
   - Impact level (high/med/low)
   - Next move (one concrete action)
5) Keep to max 3 anomalies.
6) If no anomalies, send one short line: "No major anomalies detected this morning."

Style:
- Brief, practical, no fluff.
- PT references only.

Morning reflection trigger for Ophir.

CRITICAL OUTPUT RULE: If ANY condition below is not met, produce ABSOLUTELY ZERO output. No status reports, no condition checks, no explanations, no "skipped" messages. Literally empty response. The user must NEVER see a message about why you didn't fire.

Conditions (ALL must pass):
1) America/Los_Angeles time, 6:00-10:59 AM.
2) Oura wake-up appears recent (~within 30 minutes).
3) Recent robot presence event showing it saw Ophir (within ~10 minutes).
4) Not already fired today.

If ALL conditions pass:
- POST http://172.17.0.1:8081/audio/play
- JSON: {"text":"Good morning Ophir. What are your intentions for today? What are you grateful for?"}
- If bridge is down, send ONE short failure note. That's it.

Bedtime reflection trigger for Ophir.

CRITICAL OUTPUT RULE: If ANY condition below is not met, produce ABSOLUTELY ZERO output. No status reports, no condition checks, no explanations. Literally empty response.

Conditions (ALL must pass):
1) America/Los_Angeles time, 9:30 PM.
2) Recent robot presence event showing it saw Ophir (within ~15 minutes).
3) Not already fired today.

If ALL conditions pass:
- POST http://172.17.0.1:8081/audio/play
- JSON: {"text":"Good evening Ophir. Before sleep, what did you learn today? What are you grateful for?"}
- If bridge is down, send ONE short failure note. That's it.
